question.text: Why does use of currency futures for hedging gold ETF investment NOT completely remove USDINR spot risk?

answer.text: Because futures price depends on both spot price AND premium/discount, which also changes
Because gold ETF and USDINR are unrelated
Because futures contracts are only available for 1 month
Because the lot size doesn't match the investment amount exactly

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Currency futures price = Spot + Forward premium/discount. Even if spot risk is hedged, changes in the forward premium/discount (basis risk) mean the hedge isn't perfect. **The Trap:** Lot size mismatch is a separate issue. The main reason for imperfect hedging is basis risk (changes in forward premium). **Cross-Exam Context:** Basis risk in hedging is tested in Series I. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This residual basis risk is inherent in all futures hedges — it's why forwards can sometimes provide better hedges (customized, delivery-based).
